entities.name: Professional Identity as Semantic
entities.context: Core belief that professional identity is a stable collection of meaningful stories rather than a static document, allowing truthful re-rendering into multiple forms

entities.name: Story Library
entities.context: A robust collection of atomic units of experience that candidates should build instead of polishing a static resume

entities.name: Semantic Database of Experiences
entities.context: Framework for treating individuals as databases where output can be optimized for specific receivers without violating underlying truth

entities.name: Headless CMS Analogy
entities.context: Metaphor comparing professional identity to a headless CMS where content (truth) is separate from presentation layer (resume)

entities.name: Lossy Compression of Reality
entities.context: Principle that communication inherently loses information, making canonical resumes arbitrary syntactic snapshots

entities.name: One-Page Resume
entities.context: Reframed as an output format rather than a source of truth

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company whose system should prioritize extracting atomic units of experience over simple document editing

relationships.context: Semantic identity framework implies building story libraries rather than static documents
Headless CMS serves as explanatory metaphor for the semantic database approach
Careerspan's system should prioritize extracting atomic story units from candidates
Traditional resume is repositioned as output format within the semantic identity framework
Communication loss justifies semantic over syntactic approach to identity
